summary: Patrick McKenzie announces he is quitting his day job effective March 31st to go full-time on his software businesses. He describes the mix of exhilaration and unease that comes with the transition. He updates readers on his current activities: running six simultaneous A/B tests on Bingo Card Creator, working with designers on mini-sites and button redesigns, and mentally committing to a new Twilio-based application. The post captures a pivotal moment in his transition from side-project entrepreneur to full-time independent software developer.
key_points: McKenzie is leaving his salaried position to go full-time on his software businesses, marking a major career transition
He is running six simultaneous A/B tests covering landing pages, microcopy, button designs, and signup flows — embodying his incremental optimization philosophy
Guest signup appears to have no effect on sales but generates support costs, illustrating how A/B testing reveals features worth removing
He hints at a new Twilio-based application, signaling expansion beyond Bingo Card Creator
He is systematically testing his way to growth through small 3-5% lifts rather than betting on big changes
money_quotes: I'm quitting my day job as of March 31st.

That weird not-scared-not-settled feeling one gets when one has run out the door in a hurry and is absolutely positive they have left something behind despite having verified the presence of cellphone, wallet, and day job ID card.
Oh, to no longer need the day job ID card.
I have six A/B tests currently ongoing, which I think is my personal record for simultaneous A/B tests.
Presence/absence of guest signup seems to have no effect whatsoever on sales. Good — I'm going to probably disable it, as they cost me support issues through the wazoo.
As always, I'm testing my way to victory, one 3~5% lift at a time.
key_insight: The transition from side-project to full-time entrepreneurship is built on a foundation of relentless incremental optimization — testing your way to victory one small lift at a time.
